channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: 14th Sunday of Pentecost
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: 14th Sunday of Pentecost

item.title: 14th Sunday of Pentecost, Parable Of The Wedding Feast, 2009-09-18
item.description: :14th Sunday of Pentecost. Parable Of The Wedding Feast, 2009-09-18
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :14th Sunday of Pentecost. Parable Of The Wedding Feast, 2009-09-18

item.title: 14th Sunday of Pentecost, Parable Of The Wedding Feast, It Is Always About Morality, 2010-08-29
item.description: :14th Sunday of Pentecost. The parable about the wedding feast has many layers of complex theology, and all of it is important, but as in any parable, there is something that is the most important. This is a parable about how to live the (only) way that leads to eternal life.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :14th Sunday of Pentecost. The parable about the wedding feast has many layers of complex theology, and all of it is important, but as in any parable, there is something that is the most important. This is a parable about how to live the (only) way that leads to eternal life.

item.title: 14th Sunday of Pentecost, Parable Of The Wedding Feast, The Wedding Garment Is Christ, 2011-09-18
item.description: :14th Sunday of Pentecost. Parable Of The Wedding Feast, The Wedding Garment Is Christ, 2011-09-18
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :14th Sunday of Pentecost. Parable Of The Wedding Feast, The Wedding Garment Is Christ, 2011-09-18

item.title: 14th Sunday of Pentecost, Parable Of The Wedding Feast, Abortion and praying for people in the Liturgy, 2012-09-09
item.description: :14th Sunday of Pentecost. The homily begins with a request to pray for an Orthodox woman who is planning to have an abortion. The most important place in the liturgy to offer our heartfelt prayers to God for others is given, and it is explained that among other things, the earnest of the spirit, which St Paul mentions in his epistle should motivate us for our brethren who are suffering or in danger. After a little bit about how Orthodox should view abortion, this " earnest" and the "tie in" to the parable of the wedding feast are explained, and we see how praying for others and struggling for good MUST be the result of our entry into the wedding feast, which has already occurred, and is occurring.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :14th Sunday of Pentecost. The homily begins with a request to pray for an Orthodox woman who is planning to have an abortion. The most important place in the liturgy to offer our heartfelt prayers to God for others is given, and it is explained that among other things, the earnest of the spirit, which St Paul mentions in his epistle should motivate us for our brethren who are suffering or in danger. After a little bit about how Orthodox should view abortion, this " earnest" and the "tie in" to the parable of the wedding feast are explained, and we see how praying for others and struggling for good MUST be the result of our entry into the wedding feast, which has already occurred, and is occurring.
